what_it_does: Defines a file-local decision ledger for graph-redesign forks #G1 through #G10, listing options, recommendations, gated build implications, and UNRULED RULING lines for Tim to fill before dependent implementation proceeds.
observations: States that three loop docs externalised decisions to OPEN-FOR-TIM #G1..#G10 and that this file supplies the missing ruling surface.
Frames the graph as the render slice of a broader research-first V-surface after a 2026-06-17 context shift.
Separates buildable foundations from features gated on specific rulings, then notes that even buildable foundations should wait for Tim's go under the V-surface reframe.
Each major fork presents options, a recommendation or settled constraint, and a RULING line currently set to UNRULED.
#G1 and #G1-keys define a proposed type-to-colour model keyed to real node.type strings and disjoint from six state themes.
#G4 treats TIME as an authoring-source decision before treating it as a visual-axis decision.
#G5 makes files-in-view firm while leaving the visual form open.
#G7 and #G8 are included as scoped or cross-repo follow-up decisions rather than direct layout work.
The SPEC DEBT section records implementation-guide corrections and proof-harness gaps that do not require Tim rulings.
standouts: The file uses explicit RULING lines as gates between design decisions and implementation criteria.
The V-surface reframe changes the graph work from an isolated redesign into a render slice of a larger surface.
The type-to-colour decision is tied to both visual accessibility concerns and token ownership in dna/tokens.json.
The document distinguishes decision forks from implementation debt while keeping both visible to the workflow.
conventions: Uses numbered fork headings with #G identifiers and short decision titles.
Uses option labels like (a), (b), and (c), with recommended choices marked inline.
Uses bold labels for RULING, recommended choices, and important constraints.
Uses tables for key-set coverage where exact mappings matter.
Uses status language such as Buildable NOW, Gated, Settled regardless, and BACKLOG to mark workflow state.
Breaks chronological fork order by listing #G9 and #G10 before #G7 and #G8.
concerns: Every RULING line is still UNRULED, so the file currently defines the gate surface but records no completed rulings.
SPEC DEBT mixes dispatch errata into the same file as the decision ledger, making the file serve two adjacent roles.
#G1-keys has no independent RULING line and depends on #G1, even though it is described as a correctness fix.
The fork ordering is non-sequential, which may make scanning or completion tracking less direct.
notes: Briefing view: this is the graph redesign's local ruling ledger, with unresolved forks, gating notes, and implementation errata in one place.
questions: What exact syntax should replace UNRULED when Tim rules a fork with more than one constraint or carve-out?
What artifact or condition marks that the V-surface research has landed enough for buildable foundations to proceed?
Should #G1-keys remain tied only to #G1, or should its correctness fix receive its own explicit ruling/status field?
Should SPEC DEBT remain in this decision file or move to a dispatch-oriented file once rulings are filled?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: the file provides a decision surface with options, recommendations, and RULING lines, while also carrying related status and spec-debt notes for the implementation workflow.
apparent_themes: separation of firm requirements from open presentation choices
intention_signals: It seems intended to prevent graph layout work from proceeding on unresolved assumptions by making each open fork explicit, locally ruleable, and tied to downstream completion gates.
novelty: A design-decision document that treats unresolved choices as executable workflow gates through literal RULING lines, while also recording visual-token ownership and proof debt.
contribution: Contributes a neutral decision ledger for graph-render design substrates: unresolved fork inventory, option framing, recommended paths, ruling placeholders, gating dependencies, and local implementation corrections that a fused system would preserve as decision provenance and workflow state.
summary_for_embedding: A graph-redesign OPEN-FOR-TIM decision ledger listing #G1-#G10 forks, recommended options, UNRULED ruling lines, V-surface context, gated build status, and spec-debt corrections.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for giving Tim a concrete surface to rule unresolved graph and V-surface design decisions before dependent layout, colour, edge, file-view, and dock work proceeds.
